content:
I managed to complete this with a regex including a backreference, but was confused by the wording. I'd suggest using the "package ID" (or simply "ID") wording everywhere, and abandoning the concept of "string" everywhere, i.e.:

* "To compress a package ID,"
* "If the resulting compressed package ID is strictly longer than the original package ID,"
* "[[package_id]], as a string" or equivalent in the input
* "The compressed package ID, or [[package_id]] if the compressed package ID is longer."
* "1 <= length of [[package_id]] <= 500", etc

In my opinion, it's a 10/10! I'll wait a bit before validating, in case another player has an idea for improvement... personally, I don't have any! :D

EDIT: Oh, maybe review test pairs 1 and 4 so that validator better matches the difficulty of the related test.

Thank you! Regarding your suggestion for test pairs 1 and 4, that makes total sense—aligning the validator with the specific difficulty of those tests will definitely make for a smoother experience. I will get on that and make those adjustments a bit later today.

Cases
* Each test and its validator must cover the *same scenario* with slightly different inputs and outputs. Hence, every existing case should be expanded into a pair: 1 test + 1 validator.

* Please use more randomised strings as inputs, while ensuring that all digits from 0 to 9 appear across the visible tests, and likewise across the validators.

* Please add a pair of cases where the same digit appears in separate groups within the input (e.g. 3 groups of 4 appearing in different parts of the input).

* Please ensure the cases cover the full constraint range (1 ≤ length of S ≤ 500) in a balanced way. For example, include very short inputs, medium-length inputs, and cases close to the upper bound. You may also reduce the upper bound to 100 to make modification easier.

Thanks.

Thank you for your feedback. I have updated the puzzle as requested by organizing all tests into visible and hidden pairs. I ensured that all digits from 0 to 9 are represented and added specific cases for scattered identical digits to test the counting logic more thoroughly. I also adjusted the maximum string length to 100 characters and included tests for both very short and maximum length scenarios. The puzzle should be much more robust now, thanks again for your help!

Constraints & Stub generator
* Please change 500 to 100.

-----------------------------

Thank you for revising the cases. The alignment is better now. However, the overall coverage is still insufficient. Some of the removed cases should be reinstated.

Please note that after the revision we should have 9 pairs of cases, and Pairs 4 and onwards should be ordered by increasing complexity (roughly corresponding to input length). Do not change the order of Pairs 1 to 3.

Please follow up on the points below:

Cases
* Scattered Identical Digits (Test 2): Please add at least 3 zeros at the beginning of the input to cover the leading zeros scenario, similar to Validator 2.

* Please add a pair of new cases where the input consists of a single digit.

* Please restore the case where the compressed result has the same length as the original string but is different from it (e.g. 5566 becomes 2526). Also add a new case that covers the same scenario as its counterpart.

* Please restore the case where the original string contains several groups of repeated digits and yet the compressed string is longer than the original string (e.g. input is 9988771). Also add a new case that covers the same scenario as its counterpart.

* Please add a pair of cases with medium-length **truly randomised** strings. You may use a code similar to the following to generate them:

let s = "";
while (s.length < 50) {
    let d = Math.floor(Math.random() * 10);
    let l = Math.floor(Math.random() * 10) + 1;
    s += String(d).repeat(l);
}

* Validator: Maximum Length (Validator 4): Please use this validator for a new pair of cases, and create a corresponding test where the input is also a single digit repeated 100 times.

* After that, create a new validator corresponding to "Long Mixed Sequence" (Test 4), where the input contains multiple groups of digits repeated more than 9 times.

Thanks.

Hi 5DN1L, thank you for the detailed feedback. I have fully revised the puzzle to include 9 pairs of cases as requested. Pairs 1 to 3 remain in their original order, while Pairs 4 through 9 are now ordered by increasing complexity. I updated Test 2 to include leading zeros and added a specific pair for single-digit inputs. I also reinstated and expanded the cases for equal-length compression and for scenarios where multiple groups still result in a longer compressed string. For the more complex cases, I added medium-length randomized strings and a pair for the maximum length of 100 identical digits. Finally, I included a long mixed sequence validator with groups exceeding nine identical digits. The coverage is now much more comprehensive.

Thank you very much! I agree that the coverage looks much better now.

There's just one more small fix: I noticed that the digit 8 doesn't appear in any of the validators 😆 Could you please update one of the appropriate validators to include the digit 8 in the input?

Thanks again.
Got it! ✅ Fixed! The digit 8 has been added to the validator.😊
You might also want to review the comment from @Cakeisalie5 above.
